text:
*[Bug 19093] New: download.file() and file() fail with NTLM proxy authentication when using libcurl*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19093>

            Bug ID: 19093
           Summary: download.file() and file() fail with NTLM proxy
                    authentication when using libcurl
           Product: R
           Version: R 4.5.x
          Hardware: x86_64/x64/amd64 (64-bit)
                OS: Windows 64-bit
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: major
          Priority: P5
         Component: Windows GUI / Window specific
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:Camille.PELAT@santepubliquefrance.fr|Camille.PELAT@santepubliquefrance.fr>

Created attachment 3738
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3738&action=edit>
full_download_file_log

Description
________________________________________
Context

In a Windows environment with a proxy (e.g., Squid/Olfeo) requiring NTLM
authentication, the functions download.file() and file() fail with the
following error when method = "libcurl" is used:

> download.file("<https://cran.r-project.org/src/contrib/a5R_0.4.0.tar.gz>", 
+               destfile = "a5R_0.4.0.tar.gz",
+               method = "libcurl")

Error in
download.file("<https://cran.r-project.org/src/contrib/a5R_0.4.0.tar.gz>", : 
  cannot open URL '<https://cran.r-project.org/src/contrib/a5R_0.4.0.tar.gz>'
In addition: Warning message:
In download.file("<https://cran.r-project.org/src/contrib/a5R_0.4.0.tar.gz>", : 
  URL '<https://cran.r-project.org/src/contrib/a5R_0.4.0.tar.gz>': status was
'HTTP response code said error'

________________________________________
Root Cause

The internal function curlDownload (called by both download.file() and file())
does not configure the CURLOPT_PROXYAUTH option in libcurl to enable multiple
authentication methods. By default, libcurl uses Basic Auth only, which is
rejected by the proxy. In contrast, packages like httr and pak automatically
try multiple authentication methods (including NTLM) via CURLOPT_PROXYAUTH =
CURLAUTH_ANY.

________________________________________
Evidence
1.      Logs from download.file() (see attached) show that libcurl attempts
Basic Auth only and not NTLM or other methods.
2.      Functions like httr::GET() and pak::pak() work because they
automatically try multiple authentication methods (including NTLM) via
CURLOPT_PROXYAUTH = CURLAUTH_ANY.
3.      Using method = "wininet" (via options(download.file.method =
"wininet")) bypasses the issue, as WinINet natively supports NTLM.

________________________________________
Reproducible Code for the download.file error (similar error is observed with
file())

# Fails with libcurl (Basic Auth only)
download.file(
  "<https://cran.r-project.org/src/contrib/a5R_0.4.0.tar.gz>", 
  "test.tar.gz", 
  method = "libcurl"
)

# Works with wininet (NTLM natively supported)
download.file(
  "<https://cran.r-project.org/src/contrib/a5R_0.4.0.tar.gz>", 
  "test.tar.gz",
  method = "wininet"
)

# Works with httr (automatically tries NTLM after Basic fails)
library(httr)
GET(
  "<https://cran.r-project.org/src/contrib/a5R_0.4.0.tar.gz>", 
  write_disk("test.tar.gz")
)

________________________________________
Expected Behavior

The functions download.file() and file() should support multiple proxy
authentication methods (including NTLM) when using libcurl, either by:
1.      Adding support for the proxyauth option in the extra argument (as httr
does).
2.      Setting CURLOPT_PROXYAUTH = CURLAUTH_ANY by default (to match the
behavior of httr and pak).
3.      Clearly documenting the need to use method = "wininet" for NTLM proxies
on Windows (but the wininet option is bound to be deprecated soon).

________________________________________
Environmental Information

# Output of sessionInfo()
R version 4.5.3 (2026-03-11 ucrt)
Platform: x86_64-w64-mingw32/x64
Running under: Windows 11 x64 (build 26100)

Description
________________________________________
Context

In a Windows environment with a proxy (e.g., Squid/Olfeo) requiring NTLM
authentication, the functions download.file() and file() fail with the
following error when method = "libcurl" is used:

'
In addition: Warning message:
In download.file("

': status was
'HTTP response code said error'

________________________________________
Root Cause

The internal function curlDownload (called by both download.file() and file())
does not configure the CURLOPT_PROXYAUTH option in libcurl to enable multiple
authentication methods. By default, libcurl uses Basic Auth only, which is
rejected by the proxy. In contrast, packages like httr and pak automatically
try multiple authentication methods (including NTLM) via CURLOPT_PROXYAUTH =
CURLAUTH_ANY.

________________________________________
Evidence
1.      Logs from download.file() (see attached) show that libcurl attempts
Basic Auth only and not NTLM or other methods.
2.      Functions like httr::GET() and pak::pak() work because they
automatically try multiple authentication methods (including NTLM) via
CURLOPT_PROXYAUTH = CURLAUTH_ANY.
3.      Using method = "wininet" (via options(download.file.method =
"wininet")) bypasses the issue, as WinINet natively supports NTLM.

________________________________________
Reproducible Code for the download.file error (similar error is observed with
file())

# Fails with libcurl (Basic Auth only)
download.file(
  "

", 
  "test.tar.gz", 
  method = "libcurl"
)

# Works with wininet (NTLM natively supported)

  "

", 
  "test.tar.gz",
  method = "wininet"

GET(
  "

", 
  write_disk("test.tar.gz")

________________________________________

The functions download.file() and file() should support multiple proxy
authentication methods (including NTLM) when using libcurl, either by:
1.      Adding support for the proxyauth option in the extra argument (as httr
does).
2.      Setting CURLOPT_PROXYAUTH = CURLAUTH_ANY by default (to match the
behavior of httr and pak).
3.      Clearly documenting the need to use method = "wininet" for NTLM proxies
on Windows (but the wininet option is bound to be deprecated soon).

________________________________________
Environmental Information

# Output of sessionInfo()
R version 4.5.3 (2026-03-11 ucrt)
Platform: x86_64-w64-mingw32/x64
Running under: Windows 11 x64 (build 26100)

text:
# Proxy-related environment variables
Sys.getenv(c("https_proxy", "http_proxy", "no_proxy"))

$https_proxy
[1] "<http://xxx:xxxx@xxxx:xxxxx/>"

$http_proxy
[1] "<http://xxx:xxxx@xxxx:xxxxx/>"

$no_proxy
[1] "xxxxxxxxxx"

________________________________________
Full Error Logs
see attached
________________________________________
Current Workarounds
1.      Force the use of WinINet for download.file() and file():
2.       options(download.file.method = "wininet", url.method = "wininet")
3.      Use httr::GET() or pak::pak() for downloads (they automatically handle
NTLM).

see attached

2.       options(download.file.method = "wininet", url.method = "wininet")
3.      Use httr::GET() or pak::pak() for downloads (they automatically handle
NTLM).

*[Bug 18944] Add a preparsing filter step to aspell() in order to make LaTex spell checking more robust*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18944>

--- Comment #5 from Gabriel Becker (<mailto:gabembecker@gmail.com|gabembecker@gmail.com>) ---
Hi Kurt,

Coming back to this, the reason we proposed a pre-processing filter is that the
filter (pipeline, as you pointed out) occurs *after* parsing, which is where
the failure is occuring, so a filter won't be able to fix the issue.

text:
*[Bug 18136] Inconsistent "scipen"-like effect (regression in R4)*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18136>

--- Comment #4 from Kevin Ushey (<mailto:kevinushey@gmail.com|kevinushey@gmail.com>) ---
As Luke noted, this is constant folding in the byte compiler. `c("asdf",
1000000)` is evaluated once at compile time and the result is stored in the
bytecode constant pool. Because combining a string with a number coerces the
number to a string, and that coercion honors `getOption("scipen")`, the string
`"1e+06"` (scipen=0 at compile time) gets frozen into the constant -- so
`scipen` has no effect at run time. Disassembly shows the folded constant
directly:

```
LDCONST  ->  c("asdf", "1e+06")
```

Two details explain why it only showed up inside a package and looked like an
R4 regression: namespace-resolved base functions (like `c` in package code) are
foldable at the default optimize level 2, whereas global ones are only folded
at level 3; and byte-compilation of packages became the default in R 3.5.0. The
same trap affects `max`/`min`, which also coerce mixed arguments to character.

Following Luke's suggestion, the fix declines to fold a call to a character
result unless every argument is already character. Folding is only an
optimization, so declining is always safe, and this invariant avoids relying on
which coercions happen to be option-dependent (double and complex use `scipen`;
integer and logical do not -- and `mode()` cannot even distinguish integer from
double, since both report "numeric"). Homogeneous non-character calls (e.g.
all-numeric) still fold as before.

```
Index: src/library/compiler/R/cmp.R
===================================================================
--- src/library/compiler/R/cmp.R        (revision HEAD)
+++ src/library/compiler/R/cmp.R        (working copy)
@@ -422,6 +422,17 @@

 constModes <- c("numeric", "logical", "NULL", "complex", "character")

+## Check whether a call combining arguments of these modes can be safely
+## constant folded.  Every argument must be of a foldable mode.  In addition,
+## folding a call (e.g. c() or max()) that produces a character result by
+## coercing non-character values (such as numbers) turns them into strings,
+## and that coercion depends on run-time options such as 'scipen'.  To keep
+## the compiled constant independent of such options, only fold to a
+## character result when every argument is already character (PR#18136).
+canConstantFoldModes <- function(modes)
+    all(modes %in% constModes) &&
+    (! ("character" %in% modes) || all(modes == "character"))
+
 constNames <- c("pi", "T", "F")

 checkConst <- function(e) {
@@ -471,7 +482,7 @@
                 else return(NULL)
             }
             modes <- unlist(lapply(args, mode))
-            if (all(modes %in% constModes)) {
+            if (canConstantFoldModes(modes)) {
                 tryCatch(checkConst(do.call(ffun, args)),
                          error = function(e) NULL, warning = function(w) NULL)
                 ## **** issue warning??
```

And a regression test in `src/library/compiler/tests/const.R`:

```
Index: src/library/compiler/tests/const.R
===================================================================
--- src/library/compiler/tests/const.R  (revision HEAD)
+++ src/library/compiler/tests/const.R  (working copy)
@@ -63,3 +63,29 @@
                     checkConst(sqrt(2))))
 stopifnot(identical(constantFold(quote(sqrt(2)), list(optimize = 0, env =
ce)),
                     NULL))
+
+## PR#18136: folding a call that coerces non-character values to strings
+## would freeze the formatting (e.g. 'scipen') at compile time, so a call is
+## only folded to a character result when every argument is already
+## character.  Homogeneous non-character calls are still folded.
+stopifnot(identical(constantFold(quote(c("asdf", 1000000)),
+                                 list(optimize = 3, env = ce)),

elements.text:

1000000)` is evaluated once at compile time and the result is stored in the
bytecode constant pool. Because combining a string with a number coerces the
number to a string, and that coercion honors
(scipen=0 at compile time) gets frozen into the constant -- so

directly:

Two details explain why it only showed up inside a package and looked like an

foldable at the default optimize level 2, whereas global ones are only folded
at level 3; and byte-compilation of packages became the default in R 3.5.0. The
same trap affects

Following Luke's suggestion, the fix declines to fold a call to a character
result unless every argument is already character. Folding is only an
optimization, so declining is always safe, and this invariant avoids relying on
which coercions happen to be option-dependent (double and complex use

;
integer and logical do not -- and

cannot even distinguish integer from

===================================================================

+## Check whether a call combining arguments of these modes can be safely
+## constant folded.  Every argument must be of a foldable mode.  In addition,
+## folding a call (e.g. c() or max()) that produces a character result by
+## coercing non-character values (such as numbers) turns them into strings,
+## and that coercion depends on run-time options such as 'scipen'.  To keep
+## the compiled constant independent of such options, only fold to a
+## character result when every argument is already character (PR#18136).
+canConstantFoldModes <- function(modes)
+    all(modes %in% constModes) &&
+    (! ("character" %in% modes) || all(modes == "character"))

 constNames <- c("pi", "T", "F")

 checkConst <- function(e) {
@@ -471,7 +482,7 @@
                 else return(NULL)
             }
             modes <- unlist(lapply(args, mode))
-            if (all(modes %in% constModes)) {

                 tryCatch(checkConst(do.call(ffun, args)),
                          error = function(e) NULL, warning = function(w) NULL)

```
Index: src/library/compiler/tests/const.R
===================================================================
--- src/library/compiler/tests/const.R  (revision HEAD)
+++ src/library/compiler/tests/const.R  (working copy)
@@ -63,3 +63,29 @@
                     checkConst(sqrt(2))))
 stopifnot(identical(constantFold(quote(sqrt(2)), list(optimize = 0, env =
ce)),
                     NULL))
+
+## PR#18136: folding a call that coerces non-character values to strings

+stopifnot(identical(constantFold(quote(c("asdf", 1000000)),
+                                 list(optimize = 3, env = ce)),

text:
+                    NULL))
+stopifnot(identical(constantFold(quote(c("a", 1L)),
+                                 list(optimize = 3, env = ce)),
+                    NULL))
+stopifnot(identical(constantFold(quote(c("a", TRUE)),
+                                 list(optimize = 3, env = ce)),
+                    NULL))
+stopifnot(identical(constantFold(quote(max(1000000, "a")),
+                                 list(optimize = 3, env = ce)),
+                    NULL))
+stopifnot(identical(constantFold(quote(c(1, 2, 3)),
+                                 list(optimize = 3, env = ce)),
+                    checkConst(c(1, 2, 3))))
+stopifnot(identical(constantFold(quote(c(1L, TRUE)),
+                                 list(optimize = 3, env = ce)),
+                    checkConst(c(1L, TRUE))))
+stopifnot(identical(constantFold(quote(c("a", "b")),
+                                 list(optimize = 3, env = ce)),
+                    checkConst(c("a", "b"))))
```

With this applied, `c("asdf", 1000000)` is left as a run-time call and `scipen`
is honored again (scipen=0 -> `"1e+06"`, scipen=100 -> `"1000000"`), both
inside and outside packages. The full `compiler` test suite passes.

+                                 list(optimize = 3, env = ce)),

+                                 list(optimize = 3, env = ce)),
```

text:
*[Bug 18136] Inconsistent "scipen"-like effect (regression in R4)*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18136>

--- Comment #5 from Kevin Ushey (<mailto:kevinushey@gmail.com|kevinushey@gmail.com>) ---
Slightly more readable (IMHO).

```
Index: src/library/compiler/R/cmp.R
===================================================================
--- src/library/compiler/R/cmp.R        (revision HEAD)
+++ src/library/compiler/R/cmp.R        (working copy)
@@ -422,6 +422,23 @@

 constModes <- c("numeric", "logical", "NULL", "complex", "character")

+## Check whether a call combining arguments of these modes can be safely
+## constant folded.  Every argument must be of a foldable mode.  In addition,
+## folding a call (e.g. c() or max()) that produces a character result by
+## coercing non-character values (such as numbers) turns them into strings,
+## and that coercion depends on run-time options such as 'scipen'.  To keep
+## the compiled constant independent of such options, only fold to a
+## character result when every argument is already character (PR#18136).
+canConstantFoldModes <- function(modes) {
+
+    if (!all(modes %in% constModes))
+        return(FALSE)
+
+    if ("character" %in% modes)
+        return(all(modes == "character"))
+
+    TRUE
+
+}
+
 constNames <- c("pi", "T", "F")

 checkConst <- function(e) {
@@ -471,7 +488,7 @@
                 else return(NULL)
             }
             modes <- unlist(lapply(args, mode))
-            if (all(modes %in% constModes)) {
+            if (canConstantFoldModes(modes)) {
                 tryCatch(checkConst(do.call(ffun, args)),
                          error = function(e) NULL, warning = function(w) NULL)
                 ## **** issue warning??
```

+canConstantFoldModes <- function(modes) {
+

text:
*[Bug 16629] Infinite recursion when S4 object has S3 superclass and a slot of type call*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=16629>

--- Comment #6 from Gabriel Becker (<mailto:gabembecker@gmail.com|gabembecker@gmail.com>) ---
I've confirmed that this bug is still fresh and the patch can still be cleanly

text:
*[Bug 19090] R CMD build fails on package w/o R/ directory*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19090>

Gabriel Becker (<mailto:gabembecker@gmail.com|gabembecker@gmail.com>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
             Status|UNCONFIRMED                 |TRIAGED
                 CC|                            |gabembecker@gmail.com
     Ever confirmed|0                           |1

--- Comment #3 from Gabriel Becker (<mailto:gabembecker@gmail.com|gabembecker@gmail.com>) ---
I can confirm that this behavior is now fixed under a fully updated R devel:

```
gbecker@Gabriels-MacBook-Pro gabe % mkdir pkg
echo -e 'Package: test\nVersion: 1.0.0\n' > pkg/DESCRIPTION
touch pkg/NAMESPACE
gbecker@Gabriels-MacBook-Pro gabe % ~/local/Rdevelraw/R.framework/Resources/R
CMD build pkg
* checking for file ‘pkg/DESCRIPTION’ ... OK
* preparing ‘test’:
* checking DESCRIPTION meta-information ... OK
* checking for LF line-endings in source and make files and shell scripts
* checking for empty or unneeded directories
* building ‘test_1.0.0.tar.gz’

gbecker@Gabriels-MacBook-Pro gabe % 
```

So this issue can be closed

text:
*[Bug 19091] as.numeric on macOS aarch64 rounds "0.9999999999999999" to 1.0 instead of the nearest IEEE 754 double*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19091>

Gabriel Becker (<mailto:gabembecker@gmail.com|gabembecker@gmail.com>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |gabembecker@gmail.com

--- Comment #1 from Gabriel Becker (<mailto:gabembecker@gmail.com|gabembecker@gmail.com>) ---
Michael Chirico and I have confirmed that this behavior is present on an M3
macbook pro and *not* present on 86_64 linux.

```
Copyright (C) 2025 The R Foundation for Statistical Computing
Platform: aarch64-apple-darwin20

<snip>

> sprintf("%a", as.numeric("0.9999999999999999"))
[1] "0x1p+0"
```

Note the as.numeric step is not required:

```
> sprintf("%a", 0.9999999999999999)
[1] "0x1p+0"
```

I also confirmed the python3 code provided in the report does give the IEEE
round-to-nearest result as claimed.

text:
*[Bug 18495] Alternative algorithm for `which()`*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18495>

Kylie Bemis (<mailto:kuwisdelu@gmail.com|kuwisdelu@gmail.com>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
             Status|UNCONFIRMED                 |TRIAGED
                 CC|                            |kuwisdelu@gmail.com
     Ever confirmed|0                           |1

--- Comment #8 from Kylie Bemis (<mailto:kuwisdelu@gmail.com|kuwisdelu@gmail.com>) ---
The above timings and memory improvements are still valid for current R-devel
90193.

The branchless patch is simpler, and provides real speedup for the randomized
logical vector case, but does not improve memory usage. (Note: I couldn't apply
the branchless patch directly, perhaps due to some divergence since 2023, but
it's easy enough to make the changes manually. I'll attach a new version of
this patch for reference.)

The alternative which() implementation provided by Davis improves speed in the
randomized case and real memory improvements. The code makes sense to me.

All tests pass locally, and I built and checked a small selection of CRAN
packages: __abind__, __bit64__, __data.table__, __dplyr__, and __stringi__.
These also passed. That's about as far as I can take this without input from
R-core.

Further details here: <https://github.com/r-devel/r-dev-day/issues/158>
